title: Corkscrew tissue anchor
abstract: A tissue anchor includes a driver head associated with a proximal portion of the tissue anchor, a pointed tip portion associated with a distal portion of the tissue anchor, a tissue- engagement portion disposed between the driver head and the pointed tip portion, and a cover configured to cover at least a portion of the tissue-engagement portion.
claims: Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A tissue anchor comprising: a driver head associated with a proximal portion of the tissue anchor; a pointed tip portion associated with a distal portion of the tissue anchor; a tissue-engagement portion disposed between the driver head and the pointed tip portion; and a cover configured to cover at least a portion of the tissue-engagement portion. 2. The tissue anchor of claim 1, wherein the pointed tip portion is disposed within the cover. 3 . The tissue anchor of claim 1 or claim 2, wherein the cover comprises cloth. 4 . The tissue anchor of any of claims 1-3, wherein the cover comprises polymer film. 5. The tissue anchor of any of claims 1-4, further comprising a swivel feature. 6 . The tissue anchor of claim 5, wherein the swivel feature includes a suture-holding feature. 7. The tissue anchor of claim 5 or claim 6, wherein the swivel feature comprises a fastener configured to fasten to one or more of the cover and target biological tissue. 8. The tissue anchor of any of claims 1-7, further comprising a radiopaque material associated with the cover. 9. The tissue anchor of any of claims 1-8, wherein the tissue-engagement portion has a helical form. 10. The tissue anchor of any of claims 1-9, wherein the cover is configured to bunch up as the helical tissue-engagement portion passes through the cover and into tissue. 11. The tissue anchor of any of claims 1-10, wherein the helical tissue-engagement portion is configured to pass through the cover as the driver head is rotated and pressed against biological tissue. 12. The tissue anchor of claim 11, wherein the pointed tip portion is configured to puncture the cover and at least partially pass therethrough when pressed against the biological tissue. 13. The tissue anchor of any of claims 1-12, wherein the tissue-engagement portion comprises one or more barbs. 14. The tissue anchor of any of claims 1-13, wherein the tissue-engagement portion comprises a hypodermic tube. 15. The tissue anchor of any of claims 1-14, further comprising a suture that is secured to one or more of the driver head and the helical tissue-engagement portion. 16. A method for anchoring into tissue, said method comprising: approximating a tissue anchor to tissue, the tissue anchor being at least partially encapsulated in a cover; engaging a driver head portion of the tissue anchor; puncturing at least one of the cover of the tissue anchor and the tissue with a pointed tip portion of the tissue anchor; and rotating the driver head portion to advance at least a portion of a corkscrew portion of the tissue anchor into the tissue. 17. The method of claim 16, further comprising: providing a catheter containing the tissue anchor; delivering the catheter into a ventricle of a heart associated with the tissue; and expelling the tissue anchor from the catheter. 18. The method of claim 17, further comprising carrying another tissue anchor having another cover within the catheter, wherein the cover of the other tissue anchor prevents entanglement of another corkscrew portion of the other tissue anchor with a suture coupled to the tissue anchor. 19. The method of any of claims 16-18, further comprising visually determining that the corkscrew portion of the tissue anchor has advanced into the tissue from external to a body associated with the tissue. 20. The method of claim 19, wherein said visually determining involves identifying one or more radiopaque markings associated with the cover using X-ray imaging. 21. The method of any of claims 16-20, wherein said rotating the driver head portion causes the cover to gather between the tissue and the driver head. 22. A system comprising: a delivery catheter; a corkscrew tissue anchor having a proximal head portion and a distal pointed portion; a suture coupled to the corkscrew tissue anchor; and a cover covering at least part of the corkscrew tissue anchor. 23. The system of claim 22, wherein the cover includes one or more visual markers identifiable using radiation imaging. 24. The system of claim 22 or claim 23, further comprising a swivel fastener coupled to the corkscrew tissue anchor and configured to fasten to the cover.
